title: Droplet Digital PCR Is a Novel Screening Method Identifying Potential Cardiac G-Protein-Coupled Receptors as Candidate Pharmacological Targets in a Rat Model of Pressure-Overload-Induced Cardiac Dysfunction.
author: Sayour, Navil V
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Sayour, N. V., Tóth, V. É., Nagy, R. N., Vörös, I., Gergely, T. G., Onódi, Z., Nagy, N., Bödör, C., Váradi, B., Ruppert, M., et al. (2023). Droplet Digital PCR Is a Novel Screening Method Identifying Potential Cardiac G-Protein-Coupled Receptors as Candidate Pharmacological Targets in a Rat Model of Pressure-Overload-Induced Cardiac Dysfunction. International Journal of Molecular Sciences, 24(18), 13826. DOI: 10.3390/ijms241813826 This article employs droplet digital PCR to screen 288 cardiac G-protein-coupled receptors in a rat model of heart failure, identifying potential therapeutic targets.
